original_abstract:
This research explores the vital role of education as a foundational pillar in determining a nation’s domestic and international competitiveness. Amidst fluctuations in Indonesia's global competitive standing, this study investigates how educational quality and accessibility influence human resource development. Utilizing a library research methodology and descriptive analysis, the author synthesized various academic sources, journals, and government regulations to map the correlation between pedagogical systems and national strength. The findings indicate that education serves as a strategic instrument for improving human resource quality, fostering innovation, and driving economic growth. The paper highlights that while Indonesia's educational index still lags behind some ASEAN neighbors, ongoing reforms—such as the "Kampus Merdeka" initiative and the re-evaluation of final project requirements—represent critical shifts toward a more practical, quality-driven system. Ultimately, the study concludes that enhancing educational equity and standards is essential for producing highly competitive individuals capable of navigating the demands of the global market.

**Keywords:** Relations; Education; Nation Competitiveness.
humanized_abstract:
The paper focused on several aspects of how education became one of the most important foundation of a country’s strength in term of internally and externally competing with other countries in the era of globalisation. The position of Indonesia competitiveness in Global Competitive Index from year to year faced the ups and downs, and the education quality and equality became many researchers’ object to analyse their effect toward of human resources development. This study used library research with a descriptive method and analysis, by synthezising numbers of literature from both domestic and international sources that are in form of journals, books, policies and researches. The purpose of this paper is to know how to improve our human resource quality so that they are able to contribute to the world classcompetitiveness achievement through education that develops creativity that is necessary to support the acceleration of national development. A number of country’s policies indicate the country is still needed improvement in terms of education, based on Global Competitiveness Index 2015-2016 reported by World Economic Forum, Indonesia educational index needs improvement, on the next year a number of universities changed their way of learning through “Kampus Merdeka“ program and even in this year the policy of final project of students at each educational institutions is re-evaluated, it means needs to become more practical and high quality. And therefore educational equity and quality need to be improved so as to have competitive human resources who are able to face a challenge in global market.

**Keywords:** Relations; Education; Nation Competitiveness.
